starterFiles.python:
"""build-web-scraper-python cumulative authorized crawler and accessible site-audit portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python policy, URL, response, tree, frontier, record,
and report models with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Sockets, DNS, public or private
sites, credentials, host files, native HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright browsers,
accessibility claims, legal review, load, schedules, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlPolicy:
    owner_authorization_id: str
    allowed_origins: tuple[str, ...]
    maximum_pages: int = 240
    maximum_response_bytes: int = 2_000_000
    minimum_origin_spacing_seconds: float = 1.0


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlEvidence:
    revision: str
    policy_digest: str
    frontier_digest: str
    report_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Authorized Python crawler portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Worked focus: scraper-unicode-idna-boundary — Keep display and network host forms distinct, validate Unicode and IDNA conversions, and make confusable or failed conversions visible.
# Evidence: scraper-worked-scraper-url-identity-canonicalization-5
# Competency: scraper-worked-scraper-url-identity-canonicalization-5.
# Case 9036: a multilingual public-service site review.
# Operating constraint: the prior valid checkpoint and report must survive failure.
# Changed case: introduce a block, private field, dangerous destination, or spreadsheet formula and stop or reject it.
def worked_scraper_url_identity_canonicalization_5(references: int = 24, resolved: int = 24, fetch_keys: int = 18, unsafe_merges: int = 0, idna_failures_visible: int = 2) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked5 = "worked5-5dekrow-9036"
    assert evidence_variant_worked5
    evidence_axes_worked5 = (
        "authorizationworked5",
        "url9036",
        "response5dekrow",
        "treeworked59036",
        "frontier9036worked5",
        "report5dekrow9036",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked5)) == 6
    if references < 1 or resolved < 0:
        return False, resolved, "url-count"
    if resolved != references:
        return False, resolved, "resolution-gap"
    if fetch_keys < 1 or fetch_keys > resolved:
        return False, fetch_keys, "identity-key"
    if unsafe_merges > 0 or idna_failures_visible < 1:
        return False, unsafe_merges, "url-security"
    assert resolved == references and unsafe_merges == 0
    return True, fetch_keys, "conservative-url-identity"

# Evidence: scraper-unicode-idna-boundary-ada3e
# Competency: scraper-unicode-idna-boundary.
# Case 1967: a local-server failure rehearsal.
# Operating constraint: all network and parser work must remain below explicit budgets.
# Changed case: change one base URL, fragment, query, Unicode host, or redirect and reapply scope.
def evidence_scraper_unicode_idna_boundary_ada3e(references: int = 24, resolved: int = 24, fetch_keys: int = 18, unsafe_merges: int = 0, idna_failures_visible: int = 2) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_ada3e = "ada3e-e3ada-1967"
    assert evidence_variant_ada3e
    evidence_axes_ada3e = (
        "authorizationada3e",
        "url1967",
        "responsee3ada",
        "treeada3e1967",
        "frontier1967ada3e",
        "reporte3ada1967",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_ada3e)) == 6
    if references < 1 or resolved < 0:
        return False, resolved, "url-count"
    if resolved != references:
        return False, resolved, "resolution-gap"
    if fetch_keys < 1 or fetch_keys > resolved:
        return False, fetch_keys, "identity-key"
    if unsafe_merges > 0 or idna_failures_visible < 1:
        return False, unsafe_merges, "url-security"
    assert resolved == references and unsafe_merges == 0
    return True, fetch_keys, "conservative-url-identity"

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The Unicode Idna Boundary structural check is only one gate. URL resolution and identity pipeline must also survive the stated changed case and causal explanation.

hints: For Authorized crawling and Python Build Unicode Idna Boundary evidence · Theory Unicode Idna Boundary, name the owner, authorization, stakeholder, revision, runtime, dependency, seed, URL, origin, robots, response, parser, selector, record, frontier, privacy, report, stop, recovery, and transfer boundary, Unicode Idna Boundary precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Authorized crawling and Python Build Unicode Idna Boundary evidence · Theory Unicode Idna Boundary, trace this concrete input and predict authorization, URL admission, robots, fetch, redirect, decode, parse, extract, provenance, schedule, report, rejection, stop, rollback, recovery, and native evidence before reviewing it: A theory site-quality team handles case 2970 during a multilingual public-service site review. Build deterministic Python evidence to reconcile URL parsing, base resolution, fragment handling, conservative fetch identity, and Unicode host evidence; the target owner must be able to pause the crawl; then replace a static fixture with an explicitly authorized dynamic case and restate the browser budget. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, TLS, HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright, browsers, files, credentials, target sites, accessibility evaluation, privacy or legal review, load, schedulers, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Keep display and network host forms distinct, validate Unicode and IDNA conversions, and make confusable or failed conversions visible.

For Authorized crawling and Python Build Unicode Idna Boundary evidence · Theory Unicode Idna Boundary, challenge the documented misconception with changed-authorization, URL, robots rule, response, encoding, markup, selector, frontier, timing, session, browser, privacy, security, export, failure, recovery, and controlled owner transfer evidence: Unicode hostnames and their ASCII forms are interchangeable without validation.

steps.title: Authorized crawling and Python Defend the Unicode Idna Boundary model · Theory Unicode Idna Boundary
steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Unicode Idna Boundary: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory site-quality team handles case 3875 during a local-server failure rehearsal. Build deterministic Python evidence to reconcile URL parsing, base resolution, fragment handling, conservative fetch identity, and Unicode host evidence; no block, CAPTCHA, identity, or fingerprint evasion is permitted; then change malformed markup or parser choice and preserve extraction provenance. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, TLS, HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright, browsers, files, credentials, target sites, accessibility evaluation, privacy or legal review, load, schedulers, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Keep display and network host forms distinct, validate Unicode and IDNA conversions, and make confusable or failed conversions visible.

steps.why: Retrieving Unicode Idna Boundary through the concrete URL resolution and identity pipeline case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Keep display and network host forms distinct, validate Unicode and IDNA conversions, and make confusable or failed conversions visible.
